content:
What are the Top Innovation Grants in the Prairies?

{/* PrairiesCan Funding */}
PrairiesCan - Prairies Economic Development Canada
Up to $10M
Regional Development
Repayable

Federal regional development agency supporting innovation, business growth, and economic diversification across Manitoba, Saskatchewan, Alberta, and parts of BC with comprehensive funding programs.

Program Focus:
  • • Innovation commercialization
  • • Business scale-up and expansion
  • • Technology development
  • • Regional economic development
Support Features:
  • • Up to 50% of eligible project costs
  • • Repayable contributions
  • • Multi-year project support
  • • Export market development
{/* Saskatchewan Innovation */}
Saskatchewan Innovation Programs
Up to $3M
Applied Research
Cost-Share

Saskatchewan's innovation ecosystem including Innovation Place, Saskatchewan Polytechnic Applied Research, and provincial funding supporting technology commercialization and applied research.

Innovation Support:
  • • Innovation Place tech parks
  • • Applied research programs
  • • Commercialization support
  • • Industry partnerships
Sector Strengths:
  • • Agricultural technology
  • • Mining innovation
  • • Energy & uranium technology
  • • Water & environmental tech
{/* Manitoba Research */}
Manitoba Research & Innovation Programs
Up to $2M
Technology Dev
Various Terms

Manitoba innovation support including Research Manitoba, Mitacs programs, university partnerships, and sector-specific funding for technology development and commercialization.

Research Support:
  • • Research Manitoba programs
  • • University-industry partnerships
  • • Technology commercialization
  • • Infrastructure access
Innovation Focus:
  • • Clean technology & renewable energy
  • • Advanced manufacturing
  • • AgriFood & processing
  • • ICT & digital innovation
{/* AgriFood Innovation Programs */}
Prairie AgriFood Innovation Programs
Up to $5M
AgriTech
Sector-Specific

Comprehensive agri-food technology support through federal Canadian Agricultural Partnership (CAP), provincial programs, and Prairie-specific initiatives for precision agriculture and food processing innovation.

AgriTech Areas:
  • • Precision agriculture & automation
  • • Crop genetics & breeding
  • • Livestock management systems
  • • Food processing technology
Funding Programs:
  • • CAP cost-share programs
  • • Provincial agri-innovation funds
  • • Research facility access
  • • Commercialization support
shortAnswer: Yes — Complete guide to Prairie innovation grants. Access $580M+ through Saskatchewan Innovation, Manitoba Research, PrairiesCan funding, and agri-food, mining technology, renewable energy programs. Funding available: up to $580M+.
shortAnswerQuestion: What innovation and technology grants are available in 2026?

faq.question: What is the application deadline for Prairie Provinces Innovation Grants  | + AgriFood, Mining & Renewable Energy Funding in 2026?
faq.answer: Deadlines typically vary by specific program tier. However, for 2026, many initiatives operate on a rolling intake basis until funds are depleted. Check the official portal for specific cutoffs.

faq.question: How much funding can I get through Prairie Provinces Innovation Grants  | + AgriFood, Mining & Renewable Energy Funding?
faq.answer: Funding amounts range depending on business size and scope. Top awards can reach $580M.

faq.question: Do I have to give up equity for this program?
faq.answer: No. Government grants and subsidies are non-dilutive, meaning you retain 100% ownership of your company.

expertTip.title: Resource Conversion
expertTip.content: Prairie provinces prioritize resource conversion projects. Agricultural and mining tech that adds value to raw materials gets highest priority.
